data.body: (d) RE-REVIEW RESULT (fixed prompts, venv repointed to worktree): GATE FIX CONFIRMED. S1 went BLOCK->PASS; S2 now has ZERO 'doesn't-exist' findings (previously dinged for wipChain etc.) — the consumer-references-dependency-deliverable false positives are cleared. epic/S1/S6 PASS, signed. Residual BLOCKs on S2/S4/S5 are NOT the fixed bug; they split into: (1) real inconsistencies my critical-review (b) edits introduced — S2 Dependencies still says 'autolander-bot vote ACL' after I widened S1's ACL to Contributors; S4 references S5's heartbeat without an S4->S5 dep; 'CONTRIBUTING §FFO' cited but no such section; (2) VALID forward-ref-to-DOWNSTREAM findings the gate CORRECTLY keeps — S4->ADR-0042 (S6 is downstream of S4), exactly the 'don't reference until created' rule; (3) a real ambiguity — 'submittable=True' (REST label field, true per 511) vs ADR-0040's 'submittable only on tip' (effective landability) needs disambiguation; (4) real design findings — S2 singular tested_sha can't hold a multi-member chain's SHAs; (5) over-specification (exact schemas/timeouts/vocab) appropriately declinable; (6) legit grounding vs EXISTING files — S5 fetch-secrets.sh/observability.sh not listed as edit targets. A final targeted round would fix (1)(2)(3)(4)(6) and decline (5).
